calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Amendment To The First Account
Off Calendar Aug-12-2009 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Amendment To The First Account
Off Calendar Jul-29-2009 Continued To Aug-12-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Jun-18-2009 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar Jun-29-2009 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky for Pearle Morgan. Randall Schmidt and Christine Hoburg for and with Earlie Jones.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. The Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted with 30 days leave to amend. The July 10,2009 trial date is off calendar. Status on filing of the amendment to the First Account was set for July 29, 2009. Mr. Schmidt will prepare the order, present it to Mr. Skootsky and submit it to the Court.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Mar-25-2009 Continued To Jul-10-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky for Pearlie Morgan (Respondent). Christine Hoburg for Earlie Jones (Petitioner/Trustee).

Mr. Skootsky stated that they had agreed to a plan to get the Sisters to participate in mediation and requested an order from the Court referring them to mediation. The Court denied Mr. Skootsky's request and stated that the case needed to be resolved. In addition, the Court pointed out that there was no cash to pay for Attorney's fees and asked Counsel to look into the issue regarding the Court having authority to order sale of the property. The matter was set for trial on the issues raised in the first account and will be heard on July 10, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Trial briefs and any pre-trial motions are due by July 2, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Jackson.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Mar-18-2009 Continued To Mar-25-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel An Accounting, Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar Jul-20-2006 Denied

Pet. To Determine Validty Of Purported Trust
Off Calendar Jul-20-2006 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Barbara Jackson appearing for and with Pearle Morgan. Phyllis Andelin appearing for and with respondent Earlie Jones. Both sides agree to submit the matter on pleadings. Matter submitted. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Teena Paxton CSR#1558. Clerk: Ella Yip.

Pet. To Determine Validty Of Purported Trust
Off Calendar May-22-2006 Continued To Jul-17-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel An Accounting, Breach Of Trust
Off Calendar May-22-2006 Continued To Jul-17-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Pet. To Determine Validty Of Purported Trust
Off Calendar Mar-27-2006 Continued To May-22-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Barbara Jackson appearing for petitioner. Carolyn Farren appearing for and with respondent, Earlie Jones. Oral arguments presented by both counsel. Court wants an amended petition in 10 days. Petitioner will need to clarify what she wants. Her appearance is required on 5/22. Matter is continued to 5/22. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

Pet. To Determine Validty Of Purported Trust
